heading: Small business energy efficiency
label: 657h Small business energy efficiency
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (e) of amended .section 1203 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1203 of Pub. L. 110–140section 638 of this titleSection was enacted as part of the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007, and not as part of the Small Business Act which comprises this chapter.
Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2
amendments: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (e) of amended .section 1203 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1203 of Pub. L. 110–140section 638 of this titleSection was enacted as part of the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007, and not as part of the Small Business Act which comprises this chapter.

children.text: the terms “Administration” and “Administrator” mean the Small Business Administration and the Administrator thereof, respectively;
the term “association” means the association of small business development centers established under ;section 648(a)(3)(A) of this title
the term “disability” has the meaning given that term in ;section 12102 of title 42
the term “Efficiency Program” means the Small Business Energy Efficiency Program established under subsection (c)(1);
the term “electric utility” has the meaning given that term in ;section 2602 of title 16
the term “high performance green building” has the meaning given that term in ;section 17061 of title 42

the term “on-bill financing” means a low interest or no interest financing agreement between a small business concern and an electric utility for the purchase or installation of equipment, under which the regularly scheduled payment of that small business concern to that electric utility is not reduced by the amount of the reduction in cost attributable to the new equipment and that amount is credited to the electric utility, until the cost of the purchase or installation is repaid;
the term “small business concern” has the same meaning as in ;section 632 of this title
the term “small business development center” means a small business development center described in ;section 648 of this title
the term “telecommuting” means the use of telecommunications to perform work functions under circumstances which reduce or eliminate the need to commute;
the term “Telecommuting Pilot Program” means the pilot program established under subsection (d)(1)(A); and
the term “veteran” has the meaning given that term in .section 101 of title 38

children.heading: Implementation of small business energy efficiency program
children.label: b Implementation of small business energy efficiency program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Administrator shall promulgate final rules establishing the Government-wide program authorized under subsection (d) of that ensure compliance with that subsection by not later than 6 months after .December 19, 2007December 19, 2007section 6307 of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: becoming more energy efficient;

children.label: B
children.text: understanding the cost savings from improved energy efficiency; and

children.label: C
children.text: identifying financing options for energy efficiency upgrades.

children.label: A
children.text: in consultation with the Secretary of Energy and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: in cooperation with any entities the Administrator considers appropriate, such as industry trade associations, industry members, and energy efficiency organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: small business concerns, including smaller design, engineering, and construction firms; and

children.label: B
children.text: other Federal programs for energy efficiency, such as the Energy Star for Small Business program.

children.heading: Strategy required
children.label: A Strategy required
children.text: The Administrator shall develop a strategy to educate, encourage, and assist small business concerns in adopting energy efficient building fixtures and equipment.

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: Not later than , the Administrator shall submit to Congress a report containing a plan to implement the strategy developed under subparagraph (A).December 31, 2008

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 1 Authority
children.text: The Administrator shall establish a Small Business Energy Efficiency Program to provide energy efficiency assistance to small business concerns through small business development centers.

children.label: i
children.text: provide access to information and resources on energy efficiency practices, including on-bill financing options;

children.label: ii
children.text: conduct training and educational activities;

children.label: iii
children.text: offer confidential, free, one-on-one, in-depth energy audits to the owners and operators of small business concerns regarding energy efficiency practices;

children.label: iv
children.text: give referrals to certified professionals and other providers of energy efficiency assistance who meet such standards for educational, technical, and professional competency as the Administrator shall establish;

children.label: v
children.text: to the extent not inconsistent with controlling State public utility regulations, act as a facilitator between small business concerns, electric utilities, lenders, and the Administration to facilitate on-bill financing arrangements;

children.label: I
children.text: evaluate energy efficiency opportunities and opportunities to design or construct high performance green buildings;

children.label: II
children.text: evaluate renewable energy sources, such as the use of solar and small wind to supplement power consumption;

children.label: III
children.text: secure financing to achieve energy efficiency or to design or construct high performance green buildings; and

children.label: IV
children.text: implement energy efficiency projects;

children.label: I
children.text: technology assessment;

children.label: II
children.text: intellectual property;

children.label: III
children.text: Small Business Innovation Research submissions under ;section 638 of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: strategic alliances;

children.label: V
children.text: business model development; and

children.label: VI
children.text: preparation for investors; and

children.label: viii
children.text: help small business concerns improve environmental performance by shifting to less hazardous materials and reducing waste and emissions, including by providing assistance for small business concerns to adapt the materials they use, the processes they operate, and the products and services they produce.

children.label: i
children.text: a summary of the energy efficiency assistance provided by that center under the Efficiency Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of small business concerns assisted by that center under the Efficiency Program;

children.label: iii
children.text: statistics on the total amount of energy saved as a result of assistance provided by that center under the Efficiency Program; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any additional information determined necessary by the Administrator, in consultation with the association.

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: C Reports to Congress
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which all reports under subparagraph (B) relating to a year are submitted, the Administrator shall submit to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship of the Senate and the Committee on Small Business of the House of Representatives a report summarizing the information regarding the Efficiency Program submitted by small business development centers participating in that program.

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: 3 Eligibility
children.text: A small business development center shall be eligible to participate in the Efficiency Program only if that center is certified under .section 648(k)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall, to the maximum extent practicable, select small business development centers in such a manner so as to promote a nationwide distribution of centers participating in the Efficiency Program; and

children.label: B
children.text: may not select more than 1 small business development center in a State to participate in the Efficiency Program.

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: 5 Matching requirement
children.text: Subparagraphs (A) and (B) of shall apply to assistance made available under the Efficiency Program.section 648(a)(4) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: not less than $100,000 in each fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: not more than $300,000 in each fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: not later than 30 months after the date of disbursement of the first grant under the Efficiency Program, initiate an evaluation of that program; and

children.label: i
children.text: the results of the evaluation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any recommendations regarding whether the Efficiency Program, with or without modification, should be extended to include the participation of all small business development centers.

children.heading: Guarantee
children.label: 8 Guarantee
children.text: To the extent not inconsistent with State law, the Administrator may guarantee the timely payment of a loan made to a small business concern through an on-bill financing agreement on such terms and conditions as the Administrator shall establish through a formal rulemaking, after providing notice and an opportunity for comment.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 9 Implementation
children.text: Subject to amounts approved in advance in appropriations Acts and separate from amounts approved to carry out , the Administrator may make grants or enter into cooperative agreements to carry out this subsection.section 648(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 10 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to make grants and enter into cooperative agreements to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 11 Termination
children.text: The authority under this subsection shall terminate 4 years after the date of disbursement of the first grant under the Efficiency Program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator shall conduct, in not more than 5 of the regions of the Administration, a pilot program to provide information regarding telecommuting to employers that are small business concerns and to encourage such employers to offer telecommuting options to employees.

children.heading: Special outreach to individuals with disabilities
children.label: B Special outreach to individuals with disabilities
i
children.text: small business concerns owned by or employing individuals with disabilities, particularly veterans who are individuals with disabilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: Federal, State, and local agencies having knowledge and expertise in assisting individuals with disabilities, including veterans who are individuals with disabilities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any group or organization, the primary purpose of which is to aid individuals with disabilities or veterans who are individuals with disabilities.

children.label: i
children.text: produce educational materials and conduct presentations designed to raise awareness in the small business community of the benefits and the ease of telecommuting;

children.label: I
children.text: to small business concerns that are considering offering telecommuting options; and

children.label: II
children.text: as provided in subparagraph (B); and

children.label: iii
children.text: acquire telecommuting technologies and equipment to be used for demonstration purposes.

children.heading: Selection of regions
children.label: D Selection of regions
children.text: In determining which regions will participate in the Telecommuting Pilot Program, the Administrator shall give priority consideration to regions in which Federal agencies and private-sector employers have demonstrated a strong regional commitment to telecommuting.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 2 Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date on which funds are first appropriated to carry out this subsection, the Administrator shall transmit to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship of the Senate and the Committee on Small Business of the House of Representatives a report containing the results of an evaluation of the Telecommuting Pilot Program and any recommendations regarding whether the pilot program, with or without modification, should be extended to include the participation of all regions of the Administration.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 3 Termination
children.text: The Telecommuting Pilot Program shall terminate 4 years after the date on which funds are first appropriated to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Administration $5,000,000 to carry out this subsection.
